name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America New Orleans, Louisiana — Advance America offers same-day payday, installment, and title loans up to $1,000 ($25,000 for auto title loans...
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized lender specializing in short-term emergency cash solutions for consumers facing immediate financial needs. Founded as a major player in the subprime lending market, the company operates over 2,000 physical store locations nationwide, including this Jacksonville, FL location, and provides online lending options for accessibility.

The company offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans ($100–$500, 2–4 week terms), Installment Loans ($100–$1,000 with multiple repayment options), and Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000 through partnership with LoanCenter, using vehicle equity as collateral). All products feature same-day or next-day funding availability. The Jacksonville location operates extended hours (10am–6pm weekdays, 9am–6pm Friday, 9am–4pm Saturday) with bilingual staff and accepts customers who can provide government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and SSN/ITIN.

Advance America differentiates itself through its extensive physical footprint, allowing in-person applications and immediate funding, combined with online application options for convenience. The company emphasizes customer service, with their Jacksonville location holding a 4.9/5 star rating across 125,419+ reviews. They also offer ancillary services like Western Union money transfer at physical locations, streamlining customer transactions.

However, consumers should approach these products with caution. Payday and title loans typically carry high APRs (often 400%+ annualized), making them expensive emergency solutions best reserved for true short-term crises. The company's business model relies on high-cost credit, and while convenient, these loans can trap borrowers in debt cycles if used repeatedly. This is a legitimate lender with strong operational presence, but the products themselves are predatory by design and should not be considered long-term financial solutions.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available for all loan types
Physical locations in 2,000+ stores nationwide plus online applications for convenience
Extended store hours (10am–6pm weekdays, 9am–4pm Saturday) for working consumers
Bilingual staff (Spanish-speaking) at this Jacksonville location
No stated minimum credit score requirements, accessible to credit-challenged borrowers
Title loans up to $25,000 for consumers with vehicle equity
4.9/5 star rating based on 125,419+ customer reviews with documented positive experiences
Western Union services available at physical locations for added convenience
cons: Payday and installment loans carry high APRs (typically 400%+ annualized), making them expensive emergency solutions
Debt trap risk: short-term loan structures and high fees encourage repeat borrowing and extended debt cycles
Title loans risk vehicle repossession if payments are missed, creating major financial hardship for transportation-dependent borrowers
Limited loan amounts ($500 payday, $1,000 installment) insufficient for major emergencies despite marketing as solutions
No transparent APR or fee disclosure on landing page—cost details require store visit or phone call
best_for: Consumers with immediate $200–$500 cash needs between paychecks who can repay within 2–4 weeks without financial strain
Borrowers with poor credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit cards
Vehicle owners with significant equity seeking fast larger cash amounts ($2,000–$25,000) via title loans as absolute last resort
Unbanked or underbanked individuals who prefer in-person transactions and don't have access to online lending
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers facing genuine short-term cash emergencies ($100–$1,000) who can repay within weeks without cyclical reliance, with physical store accessibility prioritized over online-only lenders. Critical caveat: these products charge 400%+ APR and are structurally designed to encourage repeat borrowing—use only as absolute last resort before payday, and avoid title loans unless vehicle loss is acceptable outcome.
services: Installment Loans ($100–$1,000 with multiple payment schedules)
Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000 using vehicle equity as collateral, via LoanCenter partnership)
In-store same-day funding and cash disbursement
Western Union money transfer services at physical locations
Customer account login and loan management

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
